factCheckNote: Pezeshkian's conditions reported by Al Jazeera from official Iranian statements

title: Iran Fires 'Most Intense Operation' — Ghadr, Emad, Fattah Missiles at Israel
description: Iran launched what it called its 'most intense operation' of the war, firing Ghadr, Emad, Kheybar Shekan, and Fattah missiles at Tel Aviv, Haifa, Israeli military intelligence HQ, Haifa naval base, radar systems, and US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain. Israeli media reported all missiles were intercepted.
factCheckNote: Iran's launch confirmed; Israeli claim of full interception unverified by independent sources

title: WHO Warns of Toxic 'Black Rain' Over Tehran from Oil Depot Strikes
description: The WHO warned that strikes on Iranian oil depots are causing toxic 'black rain' containing benzene and toluene — known carcinogens — to fall over Tehran. The environmental crisis threatens millions of residents and adds a humanitarian dimension to the air campaign against Iranian energy infrastructure.
factCheckNote: WHO warning confirmed by UN News and CBC reporting

title: Brent Crude Closes Above $100 — IEA Announces Largest-Ever Emergency Release
description: Brent crude closed at $100.46/barrel (+9.22%), the first close above $100 since August 2022. WTI hit $95.73 (+9.72%). In response, the IEA announced its largest-ever emergency oil release: 400 million barrels from 32 countries, with the US contributing 172 million from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. Full discharge expected to take ~120 days. Markets remain skeptical the release can offset Hormuz disruption. Dow fell 739 points (-1.56%), S&P 500 -1.52%, Nasdaq -1.78%.
factCheckNote: IEA release confirmed by official IEA press release; oil prices from CNBC market data; stock figures from CNBC

title: NATO Intercepts Third Iranian Missile Over Turkey
description: NATO air defenses intercepted a third Iranian ballistic missile over Turkish airspace near Adana, close to Incirlik Air Base. Residents were woken by air raid sirens at 3:25 AM local time. Turkey condemned the repeated violations of its airspace.
factCheckNote: Turkish government and NATO confirmed the interception

title: KC-135 Crew Deaths Confirmed — All 6 Killed in Iraq Crash
description: CENTCOM confirmed all 6 crew members of the KC-135 Stratotanker that crashed near Turaibil, Iraq were killed. Reports indicate a possible mid-air collision with another KC-135, which landed at Ben Gurion with tail fin damage. Total US military deaths since Feb 28 now stands at 13 service members.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM officially confirmed all 6 crew killed

title: Explosion at Al-Quds Day Rally in Tehran — 1 Killed
description: An explosion rocked an Al-Quds Day solidarity march in Tehran, killing at least 1 person as thousands participated. The cause of the explosion remains under investigation. Al-Quds Day rallies are held annually in support of Palestinians.
factCheckNote: Explosion confirmed by Iranian state media and Al Jazeera reporting

title: Iranian Missile Strikes Zarzir, Northern Israel — 50+ Wounded
description: An Iranian missile struck Zarzir in northern Israel, moderately wounding a woman and injuring over 50 people from shrapnel and flying glass. A teen girl was also killed running to a shelter during the attack.
factCheckNote: Israeli media confirmed strike and casualties

title: Netanyahu's First Press Conference — 'Crushing' Iran
description: Netanyahu held his first press conference since the war began, declaring Israel is 'crushing' Iran and mocking Mojtaba Khamenei for not showing his face publicly. Pentagon chief Hegseth separately claimed Khamenei is 'likely disfigured' and that every Iranian ballistic missile company has been destroyed, with 15,000+ targets struck.
factCheckNote: Press conference confirmed; Hegseth's claims about Khamenei's condition and 15,000 targets not independently verified

title: 11 Iranian Cluster Missiles Penetrate Israeli Defenses — 2 Killed
description: Eleven Iranian cluster missiles penetrated Israeli air defenses, with one dropping approximately 70 cluster bombs over central Israel. Two Israelis were killed. The use of cluster munitions — banned under the 2008 Convention — marks a significant escalation.
factCheckNote: Haaretz investigation confirmed cluster missile penetration and casualty count

title: Michigan Synagogue Attack — Vehicle Rams Temple Israel
description: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali rammed a vehicle into Temple Israel synagogue near Detroit, Michigan. The suspect's relatives were reportedly killed in an Israeli strike. The attack represents a domestic spillover of the conflict onto US soil.
factCheckNote: Attack confirmed by law enforcement; suspect identified

title: Treasury Waives Russia Oil Sanctions to Ease Supply Crunch
description: The US Treasury Department waived sanctions on Russian oil exports for one month to free stranded tankers and ease the global supply crunch caused by the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The unprecedented move signals the severity of the energy crisis.
factCheckNote: Treasury waiver confirmed by Time and NBC reporting

title: All 6 KC-135 Crew Members Confirmed Dead in Iraq Crash
description: Pentagon confirms all 6 crew aboard the US Air Force KC-135 tanker that crashed in western Iraq have died. The crash involved a second KC-135 which landed at Ben Gurion with tail fin damage, suggesting a possible mid-air collision. Total US service members killed reaches 13. This is the 4th manned US aircraft lost this month.
factCheckNote: Pentagon and CENTCOM confirmed all crew deaths

title: 2,200 Marines Ordered to Middle East — Ground Operations Not Ruled Out
description: The 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit with 2,200 Marines on USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group (USS Tripoli, USS San Diego, USS New Orleans) plus ~20 F-35Bs ordered from Japan to Middle East. Pentagon refuses to rule out ground operations. Over a week transit time.
factCheckNote: Deployment confirmed by Axios and Military Times reporting from Pentagon sources

title: Highest Volume US Strikes Yet — 15,000+ Targets Hit Total
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth announces the 'highest volume of strikes' over Iran's skies, with over 15,000 enemy targets struck since Feb 28 (1,000+/day). Claims Iran's missile capacity down 90%, drone capacity down 95%. Over 60 Iranian ships and 30 minelayers eliminated.
factCheckNote: Hegseth confirmed at Pentagon press briefing. Independent verification of specific numbers limited.

title: Israel Destroys Zrarieh Bridge — First Civilian Infrastructure Strike in Lebanon
description: Israel destroys the Zrarieh Bridge over the Litani River in Lebanon, the first acknowledged strike on civilian infrastructure. Leaflets dropped over Beirut warn of 'Gaza-scale devastation.' Israeli attacks kill 25+ in Beirut, southern and eastern Lebanon, hitting areas never targeted before. Lebanon toll reaches 773 killed, 1,933 wounded, 800,000+ displaced.
factCheckNote: Bridge destruction confirmed by Al Jazeera and Lebanese sources

title: Brent Crude Closes Above $103 — Second Day Above $100
description: Brent crude closes at $103.14/barrel (+2.67%), second consecutive close above $100. WTI at $98.71 (+3.11%). Despite IEA's record 400M barrel reserve release and US waiving Russian oil sanctions, prices continue climbing. Brent up ~10% for the week.
factCheckNote: Oil prices confirmed by CNBC and commodity exchanges

title: Iran Considers Yuan-Only Hormuz Access — Geopolitical Shift
description: Iran reportedly considering allowing oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if trade is conducted in Chinese yuan, bypassing the US dollar. If implemented, this would represent a major shift in global oil trade dynamics. Iran previously allowed 2 Indian-flagged LPG carriers to pass under Indian Navy escort.
factCheckNote: Reported by Bloomberg; Iran has not officially confirmed the yuan condition

title: US Gas Prices Hit $3.63 — California Above $5/Gallon
description: US national average gas price reaches $3.63/gallon per AAA, up 35 cents in one week and 58 cents from a month ago. California surpasses $5/gallon. Analysts predict $4/gallon nationally by next week. Pre-war average was below $3.00 for 13 consecutive weeks.
factCheckNote: AAA gas price data confirmed

title: Trump Announces Massive Kharg Island Bombing Raid
description: CENTCOM strikes all military targets on Kharg Island, Iran's main oil export hub handling 90% of crude exports. More than 15 explosions reported targeting army defenses, Joshen Sea Base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared. Trump warns he will hit oil facilities next if Iran continues blocking Hormuz.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNN, CNBC, The Hill, Axios, Washington Post

title: Missile Strikes US Embassy Baghdad Helipad
description: A missile strikes the helipad inside the US Embassy compound in Baghdad. A suicide drone reportedly targeted the embassy's radar system first. The IRGC confirmed this as the '48th phase' of its retaliatory campaign. Smoke seen rising from the compound.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post, AP, Al Jazeera video

title: $10M Bounty on Mojtaba Khamenei and 7 IRGC Commanders
description: State Department announces $10 million reward for information on Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei and 7 other senior Iranian officials including IRGC commanders. Largest bounty placed on a sitting head of state since the conflict began.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Israel Hayom, Washington Examiner

title: NATO Intercepts 3rd Iranian Missile Over Turkey
description: Turkey reports NATO defenses intercepted a third Iranian missile that entered Turkish airspace. Residents of Adana near Incirlik airbase woken by sirens at 3:25 AM. Third such incident since the war began, raising tensions between NATO and Iran.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera and Bloomberg

title: 10,000 AI-Powered Merops Interceptor Drones Deployed
description: US Army deploys 10,000 Merops AI-powered interceptor drones to the Middle East. Each costs $14K-$15K, developed by Eric Schmidt-backed Perennial Autonomy and tested in Ukraine. Designed to replace $4M+ Patriot interceptors for counter-drone defense.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg

title: Hegseth: Iran's Ballistic Missile Production 'Eliminated'
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth declares Iran's entire ballistic missile production capacity has been eliminated. Iran's missile volume down 90%, drone volume down 95%. Announces March 14 will see the highest volume of US strikes yet — 'the highest number of sorties and bomber pulses.'
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Time, PBS, HotAir

title: Guterres Arrives in Beirut for Solidarity Visit
description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres arrives in Beirut as Lebanon death toll reaches 773. Calls for de-escalation and dialogue, says the conflict has caused 'immense suffering.' Visit comes as 800,000+ Lebanese are displaced and evacuation orders cover 14% of the country.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by UN News

title: IRGC Threatens UAE Cities — Major Escalation
description: IRGC warns residents of UAE cities to evacuate areas near ports and docks hosting US forces. Calls US positions 'legitimate targets.' First direct threat to a Gulf ally's civilian population. Follows Kharg Island bombing raid.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill and Al Jazeera

title: Iran Fires 3 Ballistic Missiles at Central Israel
description: At least 3 ballistic missiles launched at central Israel. No casualties reported. Sirens sounded across central Israel, smoke seen near Tel Aviv. Iran's missile capability down over 90% per Hegseth — these represent diminishing but continuing threat.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera and Times of Israel

title: Fujairah Port Oil Operations Suspended After Drone Attack
description: Drone attack and resulting fire at Fujairah port (UAE) forces suspension of oil loading operations. Fujairah was the key alternative route to Hormuz — its closure further tightens global oil supply. UAE has absorbed 1,668 strikes since war began (1,422 drones + 246 missiles).
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Gulf News

title: IDF Destroys Iran's Space Research Center and Air Defense Factories
description: Israeli strikes destroy Iran's primary space research center and air defense factories in Tehran. Combined US-Israel total: over 15,000 enemy targets struck, 7,600+ Israeli strikes in Iran and 1,100+ in Lebanon since Feb 28.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 15 coverage

title: CSIS: US War Costs Reach $16.5 Billion Through Day 12
description: Center for Strategic and International Studies updates cost estimate: $16.5B through Day 12, after $3.7B in first 100 hours and $11.3B through Day 6 (Pentagon to Congress). Daily burn rate estimated at $891M-$1.43B. Pentagon requesting $50B supplemental for munitions replenishment.
factCheckNote: CSIS official analysis, Pentagon testimony to Senate confirmed by NBC

title: KC-135 Refueling Plane Crashes in Western Iraq — 6 US Crew Killed
description: A KC-135 Stratotanker from Ohio Air National Guard's 121st Air Refueling Wing crashes in western Iraq. All 6 crew members killed. CENTCOM says cause is not hostile or friendly fire. An Iranian proxy group claims responsibility. Total US military dead now 13.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM confirmed crash and casualties; hostile fire ruled out per official statement

title: 3 F-15E Strike Eagles Downed in Friendly Fire Over Kuwait
description: Three US F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jets shot down over Kuwait in a mistaken friendly fire incident. All 6 crew members ejected safely and were recovered. Pentagon launches investigation into the incident.
factCheckNote: Pentagon confirmed friendly fire incident and safe recovery of all crew

title: Trump Rejects Ceasefire Talks — Claims Iran 'About to Surrender'
description: Trump administration rebuffs multiple ceasefire mediation attempts by Oman and Egypt. Senior White House official says 'He's not interested in that right now.' Trump tells G7 leaders Iran is 'about to surrender' and calls on China, France, Japan, South Korea, UK to send warships to reopen Strait of Hormuz.
factCheckNote: Multiple sources confirm ceasefire rejection and G7 call statements

title: Iran Selectively Reopens Strait of Hormuz for Non-Western Ships
description: IRGC allows passage through Strait of Hormuz for a Turkish cargo ship, 2 Indian LNG carriers, and a Saudi oil tanker. Strait remains 'prohibited' for US, Israeli, and Western-allied vessels. Mojtaba Khamenei vows to maintain blockade indefinitely. 150+ ships remain anchored outside strait.
factCheckNote: Multiple shipping trackers and news sources confirm selective passage

title: Iran Intelligence Ministry Secretly Contacts CIA Through Backchannel
description: Iran's intelligence ministry reaches out to CIA through a third country's intelligence service to discuss potential terms for de-escalation. Ali Larijani and FM Araghchi sought to use Oman as conduit for talks involving VP JD Vance — efforts have not yet materialized into formal negotiations.
factCheckNote: Reported by NYT and Jerusalem Post citing intelligence sources

title: Israel Plans Major Ground Invasion of Southern Lebanon
description: Israel planning massive ground operation south of the Litani River to dismantle Hezbollah infrastructure. Triggered by Hezbollah's 200+ missile coordinated barrage with Iran. Lebanese government outlaws Hezbollah military activities and announces it will arrest anyone connected to IRGC.
factCheckNote: Reported by Axios citing Israeli military officials

title: IEA Authorizes Record 400M Barrel Oil Reserve Release
description: International Energy Agency authorizes largest-ever coordinated oil reserve release of 400 million barrels. US contributing 172M barrels from Strategic Petroleum Reserve. Despite release, Brent crude remains above $100/barrel at $103.14. WTI at $98.71.
factCheckNote: IEA official announcement confirmed; prices verified via commodities markets

title: NPR/PBS Poll: 56% of Americans Oppose Iran War
description: NPR/PBS/Marist poll shows 56% of Americans oppose the Iran war. Market stress approaching 'Liberation Day' tariff shock levels. S&P 500 down 3% in March, now in red for year. Goldman Sachs pushes rate cut forecast from June to September.
factCheckNote: NPR/PBS/Marist polling methodology confirmed

title: US Strikes 90 Military Targets on Kharg Island — Oil Spared
description: CENTCOM strikes 90 military targets on Kharg Island — Iran's critical oil export hub handling 90% of crude exports. Targets include naval mine storage, missile bunkers, air defenses, naval base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar. Trump deliberately spares oil infrastructure but warns of 'immediate reconsideration' if Iran blocks Hormuz.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Washington Post, Al Jazeera, NPR, Time

title: Iran Retaliates — Missiles Hit Israel, Eilat, Gulf States
description: Iran launches retaliatory missiles targeting central Israel, Eilat (child moderately injured, 2 lightly hurt), and Jerusalem. Iran claims Khaibar missiles struck Netanyahu's office — no impact confirmed in Jerusalem. Qatar intercepts 4 ballistic missiles. Kuwait's Ahmad Al-Jaber Air Base hit by drones. Fujairah (UAE) fire after drone debris.
factCheckNote: Missile launches and Eilat casualties confirmed. Iran's claim of hitting Netanyahu's office unconfirmed.

title: Iran-Linked Hackers Hit Stryker — 60+ Hacktivist Groups Active
description: Iran-linked hacking group 'Handala' launches major cyberattack on medical devices company Stryker. Employee phones wiped worldwide, computer access blocked. CISA investigation launched. Pro-Russian group NoName057(16) teams with Iranian hacktivists to target Elbit Systems. Poland reports possible Iranian hack on nuclear research center. ~60 hacktivist groups active.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Axios, Al Jazeera, Palo Alto Unit 42, PBS

title: Hegseth: Iran Military 'Destroyed' — $10M Reward for Khamenei Info
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth claims Iran's military is 'destroyed.' Missile capability reduced 90%, drone capability down 95%. Claims new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei is 'wounded and likely disfigured.' State Dept offers $10M reward for Khamenei's location. US-Israeli strikes have hit 15,000+ targets across 200+ cities.
factCheckNote: $10M reward confirmed by State Dept. Capability reduction figures not independently verified. Khamenei condition unconfirmed.

title: UNHCR: 3.2M Iranians Displaced — Humanitarian Crisis Deepens
description: UNHCR reports 3.2 million Iranians internally displaced. 100,000 fled Tehran in first 72 hours. 56 cultural sites suffered serious structural damage. 29 clinical facilities damaged, 10 forced to close. 2.5M Afghan refugees in Iran also at risk. UNESCO urges protection of heritage sites. Lebanon: 830,000 displaced, 826 killed (Health Ministry).
factCheckNote: UNHCR displacement figures confirmed. Lebanon Health Ministry figures verified.

title: Heavy Overnight Strikes — Isfahan, Shiraz, Tehran, Dezful, Hamedan Hit
description: US-Israeli forces conduct heavy overnight bombing across Iran. 20 explosions reported near Shiraz, heavy strikes on Isfahan area, southern Tehran, Dezful Air Base, Khomein, and Hamedan. Part of the continued campaign that has now struck over 6,000 targets across Iran.

title: IRGC Launches 10 Hypersonic Missiles at Al Dhafra Air Base in UAE
description: Iran's IRGC claims it launched 10 missiles and several drones targeting US forces stationed at Al Dhafra Air Base in the United Arab Emirates. The IRGC also warned it would target any vessel linked to the US, Israel, or their allies transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

title: IRGC Vows to 'Pursue and Kill' Netanyahu
description: Iran's IRGC issues direct threat against Israeli PM Netanyahu. 'IRGC vows to pursue and kill child-killer Netanyahu if he is still alive,' Iran's IRNA news agency reports. The statement comes amid ongoing speculation about Netanyahu's wellbeing after he failed to appear publicly.

title: Iran Claims Drone Strikes on Israeli Police HQ and Gilat Satellite Center
description: Iran's army claims special police unit headquarters and the Gilat defence satellite communications centre were hit by drone attacks. Israeli officials have not confirmed these specific claims.

title: Iran FM Araghchi Sets Conditions for War's End
description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the end of the war depends on guarantees that attacks will not be repeated and payment of compensation for damage caused during the conflict. This marks the most specific set of conditions Iran has laid out for ending hostilities.

title: Trump: 'Not Ready for a Deal — Terms Aren't Good Enough'
description: President Trump tells NBC News that Iran 'wants to make a deal' but he is not ready because 'the terms aren't good enough yet.' He also calls on allies who rely on Gulf oil to send warships to help keep the Strait of Hormuz open, claiming 'many countries' would assist including China, France, Japan, South Korea, and the UK.
factCheckNote: Iran has set conditions (guarantees + compensation), not an unconditional desire to 'make a deal' as characterized. China and UK have not confirmed sending warships.

title: Casualty Update — Iran: 1,444 Killed, 18,551 Injured
description: Iran's Health Ministry reports 1,444 killed and 18,551 injured since strikes began on February 28. The Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA) estimates the true death toll may be higher at 1,858+, including both civilians and military personnel. In Lebanon, 826 have been killed including 106 children since March 2.

title: Lebanon Overnight Strikes Kill 4 — Hezbollah Intel Chief Eliminated
description: Israeli overnight strikes in southern Lebanon kill at least 4 people. The IDF announces that head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters, Hussein Makled, was killed in overnight strikes in Beirut. Axios reports Israel is planning a major ground invasion south of the Litani River.

title: Oil Closes Above $100 for Second Day — Gold Hits $5,032
description: Brent crude closes at $103.14/bbl (+29.2% from pre-war). WTI at $98.71. Gold reaches $5,032/oz. The IEA's release of 400 million barrels from strategic reserves has failed to stem prices as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. US gas averages $3.68/gallon, up 15.7% from pre-war.

title: F-35I Shot Down Over Tehran — First Stealth-vs-Manned Engagement
description: IRGC claims an IDF F-35I was shot down over Tehran airspace. The IDF confirms the engagement, describing it as the first stealth-vs-manned air combat in history. The pilot was recovered by a combat search and rescue (CSAR) team. Iran displayed wreckage on state TV; Pentagon declined to comment on specifics.
factCheckNote: Both sides confirm the engagement occurred. Details about how the aircraft was downed are disputed.

title: US Submarine Sinks Iranian Warship IRIS Dena — 87 Killed
description: A US submarine sinks the Iranian warship IRIS Dena off the coast of Sri Lanka in the Indian Ocean, killing 87 crewmen. CENTCOM confirms the engagement, stating the vessel was tracked after departing Bandar Abbas. Iran condemns the attack as targeting personnel outside the theater of operations.

title: Pentagon Identifies 6 Airmen Killed in KC-135 Crash — US Deaths Now 19
description: The Pentagon releases the names of 6 US Air Force airmen killed when their KC-135 Stratotanker aerial refueling aircraft crashed in Iraq. The cause is under investigation. Total US military deaths in the Iran war now stand at 19.

title: Pope Leo XIV Issues Direct 'Cease Fire!' Appeal to War Leaders
description: Pope Leo XIV escalates his language on the conflict, directly addressing the leaders of the United States, Israel, and Iran with a blunt 'Cease fire!' appeal. He references a recent school strike that killed children, calling the war 'a wound on humanity's conscience.'

title: Houthis Staying Out of Iran War — Weapons Depleted, Tehran Coordinated
description: Despite prior threats to join Iran's defense, the Houthis are staying out of the war. The Washington Post reports their weapons stockpile was depleted in the 2024-2025 Red Sea campaign, and the decision was coordinated with Tehran to avoid giving the US a pretext for strikes on Yemen.

title: Israel 'Running Critically Low' on Interceptors — IDF Denies
description: Semafor reports that Israel is running critically low on ballistic missile interceptors, citing US officials. The IDF denies the report, calling it 'complete fake.' Analysts note Israel has fired hundreds of Arrow and David's Sling interceptors since the war began, with US resupply flights ongoing.
factCheckNote: Semafor cites US officials; IDF categorically denies. Truth likely in between.

title: IDF Launches 'Wide-Scale' Strikes on Western Iran — 200+ Targets, Senior IRGC Commanders Killed
description: The IDF launches its largest single-day operation against Iran, hitting over 200 targets across western Iran in 20 attack waves. Senior IRGC commanders Jalali-Nasab and Shariat are confirmed eliminated. Targets include military infrastructure, missile production facilities, and command centers in the Hamedan and Isfahan regions.

title: Iran Warns Dubai and Doha Residents to Evacuate Specific Neighborhoods
description: Iran issues unprecedented warnings to residents of specific neighborhoods in Dubai and Doha to evacuate, signaling imminent retaliatory strikes against Gulf states perceived as supporting the US-Israel campaign. Dubai later reports successful interceptions of incoming projectiles over Marina and Al Sufouh districts.

title: Iran Hits Bnei Brak with Cluster Munitions — First 'Split-Warhead' MIRV Strike
description: Iranian missiles with cluster munitions strike Bnei Brak, wounding 3 civilians. This marks the first confirmed use of 'split-warhead' MIRV-type missiles against Israeli population centers, representing a significant escalation in Iran's missile capabilities and targeting of civilian areas.

title: Baghdad Airport Hit by 5 Rockets — US Embassy Urges Citizens to Leave Iraq
description: Five rockets strike Baghdad International Airport, wounding 5 people. The US Embassy issues a security alert urging all American citizens to leave Iraq immediately. The attack is attributed to Iran-aligned Iraqi militias retaliating for US involvement in strikes on Iran.

title: Iranian Drone Destroys Italian MQ-9 Reaper at Ali Al Salem Airbase, Kuwait
description: An Iranian drone strike destroys an Italian MQ-9 Reaper surveillance drone at Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait, causing a $35M loss. No casualties reported. The strike marks the first direct Iranian attack on a NATO ally's military asset in the Gulf, raising questions about the conflict's expansion beyond Israel and Iran.

title: US Offers $10M Bounty on Mojtaba Khamenei and 9 IRGC Officials
description: The State Department announces a $10 million Rewards for Justice bounty on Mojtaba Khamenei (son of Supreme Leader Khamenei) and 9 senior IRGC officials. The move targets Iran's command structure and signals US intent to hold individual leaders accountable for Iran's military operations.

title: IRGC Vows to 'Pursue and Kill' Netanyahu — Death Rumors Debunked
description: The IRGC publicly vows to 'pursue and kill' Israeli PM Netanyahu. Meanwhile, rumors of Netanyahu's death circulate on social media but are swiftly debunked by Israeli officials. The threat marks an escalation in personal targeting rhetoric from Iran's military leadership.
factCheckNote: IRGC threat confirmed by France 24. Netanyahu death rumors debunked by Israeli officials.

title: Energy Sec Wright: War Ends in 'Weeks' — F1 Cancels Bahrain & Saudi GPs
description: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright states the Iran war will end in the 'next few weeks,' signaling administration confidence. Separately, Formula 1 cancels both the Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Grands Prix, reducing the 2026 calendar to 22 races — a tangible sign of the war's impact on global events and commerce.

title: IDF Launches Ground Operation in Southern Lebanon — Enters Khiam
description: Israeli military launches its largest ground incursion into Lebanon since 2006, entering the town of Khiam in southern Lebanon with multiple armored columns. The operation marks a significant escalation on the northern front.

title: Iranian Drone Strikes Dubai Airport Fuel Tank — Flights Suspended 7+ Hours
description: An Iranian drone strikes a fuel storage tank at Dubai International Airport, the world's busiest international hub, causing a massive fire and forcing flight suspensions for over 7 hours. The attack escalates Iran's targeting of Gulf civilian infrastructure.

title: IDF Destroys Khamenei's Aircraft at Tehran's Mehrabad Airport
description: Israel confirms destruction of late Supreme Leader Khamenei's personal aircraft at Tehran's Mehrabad Airport in a precision airstrike, a symbolic blow to the Iranian regime.

title: Fujairah Oil Hub Struck by Drones for 2nd Consecutive Day
description: UAE's Fujairah oil hub is hit by Iranian drones for the second day in a row, forcing suspension of oil loading operations at one of the Gulf's key bunkering ports.

title: Abu Dhabi Missile Strike Kills Palestinian Civilian — UAE Deaths Now 7
description: A missile strike in Abu Dhabi's Al Bahia district kills a Palestinian civilian, bringing the total UAE death toll to 7 since the conflict began. The strike highlights Iran's expanding target set across the Gulf.

title: Saudi Arabia Intercepts 60+ Iranian Drones and 6 Ballistic Missiles
description: Saudi air defenses intercept over 60 Iranian drones overnight plus 6 ballistic missiles targeting al-Kharj air base. Patriot and THAAD systems engaged in the largest defensive engagement over Saudi territory during the conflict.

title: IRGC Declares USS Ford Support Network 'Legitimate Targets'
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards formally declare the USS Gerald Ford carrier support network — including supply ships, tankers, and escort vessels — as legitimate military targets, a major escalation in rhetoric against US naval forces.

title: Trump Threatens NATO Allies, Weighs Kharg Island Seizure
description: President Trump threatens NATO allies over participation in the Strait of Hormuz coalition. Administration officials reportedly weigh plans to seize Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal, which handles 90% of Iran's crude exports.

title: EU's Kallas Proposes Black Sea-Style Grain Deal for Hormuz
description: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas proposes a Black Sea grain deal-style international arrangement for the Strait of Hormuz via the UN, seeking to guarantee shipping passage through diplomatic means.

title: Russia Closes Consulate in Isfahan Amid Intensifying Strikes
description: Russia closes its consulate in Isfahan, Iran, citing safety concerns for diplomatic staff amid intensifying Israeli bombardment of the area. The closure signals growing international unease with conditions inside Iran.

title: Hezbollah Intelligence Chief Hussain Makled Killed in Beirut Airstrike
description: IDF airstrikes on Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) reportedly kill Hezbollah intelligence chief Hussain Makled. Multiple overnight strikes rock the area as Israel escalates its campaign against Hezbollah leadership targets in Lebanon.
factCheckNote: Reported by Times of Israel and Al Jazeera; IDF confirmed targeting Hezbollah intelligence infrastructure in Dahiyeh

title: Saudi Arabia Intercepts 64 Drones Over Riyadh
description: Saudi Royal Air Defense Forces intercepted and destroyed 64 drones launched toward Riyadh and the Eastern Province since early Monday morning.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Saudi Gazette and Anadolu Agency

title: Fatal Missile Strike in Abu Dhabi — 1 Killed
description: A missile struck a civilian vehicle in Abu Dhabi's Al Bahyah area, killing a Palestinian national. UAE air defenses intercepted 6 ballistic missiles and 21 drones. Cumulative UAE totals since war start: 304 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,627 drones intercepted.
factCheckNote: Reported by Gulf News

title: Iranian Missile Fragments Fall Near Jerusalem Holy Sites
description: Fragments from intercepted Iranian missiles fell near the Church of the Holy Sepulchre, the National Library near the Knesset, and East Jerusalem. One person suffered minor burns. 108 people taken to hospitals in past 24 hours.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog with photos

title: Airstrike on PMF Headquarters in Jurf al-Sakhar, Iraq
description: Air raid hit Popular Mobilization Forces headquarters in Jurf al-Sakhar, Iraq, injuring three people.
factCheckNote: Reported by Al Jazeera Day 17 roundup

title: IDF Destroys Iran Space Research Center in Tehran
description: Israeli Air Force struck and destroyed Iran's primary space research center in Tehran, used to develop anti-satellite weapons and the Chamran-1 satellite program. Also struck air defense manufacturing facilities.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Washington Times and Times of Israel

title: IDF Completes 400th Strike Wave — 7,600+ Strikes in Iran
description: Israeli Air Force completed its 400th wave of strikes in Iran (Operation Roaring Lion). Over 7,600 separate strikes conducted including 2,000 against regime HQ/assets and 4,700 against missile program. IDF planning 3 more weeks of operations.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel

title: Iran FM Araghchi Rejects Ceasefire — Reveals Russia-China Military Cooperation
description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared Hormuz 'closed to our enemies,' rejected Trump's claim Iran asked for ceasefire ('We never asked for a ceasefire'), and revealed Iran is receiving military cooperation from Russia and China.
factCheckNote: Press conference broadcast live; confirmed by Al Jazeera and NPR

title: Rockets Hit Baghdad International Airport — 5 Wounded
description: Five rockets struck Baghdad International Airport wounding 5 (4 airport staff + 1 engineer). Kataib Hezbollah claimed the attack targeting Camp Victory. A second barrage followed at midnight.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by BNO News, GMA News, Times of Israel

title: UK, Germany, Australia, Japan All Decline Hormuz Coalition
description: Germany said war 'has nothing to do with NATO.' UK PM Starmer said he's 'not prepared to join a conflict without a plan.' Australia and Japan also declined. No country has publicly committed warships to Trump's Hormuz patrol request.
factCheckNote: Multiple official statements confirmed by National Today, CNN, Japan Times

title: Pope Leo XIV Issues Strongest Ceasefire Demand Yet
description: Pope Leo XIV at Sunday noon blessing appealed for immediate cease-fire. Vatican Secretary of State Cardinal Parolin rejected 'preventive war' claim. US Cardinals McElroy and Cupich also criticized the war publicly.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by UPI, Washington Post, Newsweek

title: IDF Destroys Supreme Leader's Aircraft at Mehrabad Airport
description: Israeli Air Force destroyed the aircraft used by Iran's Supreme Leader at Mehrabad International Airport in Tehran during overnight strikes.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Alma Center daily report and Times of Israel

title: Iranian Cluster Missile Barrage Hits Rishon Lezion — 10 Locations Damaged
description: Iran fired Fajr-type missiles with cluster munition warheads at central Israel. Impacts in Rishon Lezion, Lod, and Shoham. 10 locations damaged. 30-year-old woman injured when home destroyed. President Herzog visited impact site.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, and Ynet with photos and video

title: IDF 91st Division Launches Ground Operation — Takes Khiam, Southern Lebanon
description: IDF 91st 'Galilee' Division launched ground operation in southern Lebanon. Merkava tanks and APCs advanced to Khiam and Aitaroun. 230+ Hezbollah fighters eliminated, 200+ targets struck. 450,000 reservists called up. IDF says 3+ more weeks of operations planned through Passover.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Israel Hayom, FDD

title: Hezbollah Rocket Strikes Nahariya Residential Building — 6 Wounded Including 4 Children
description: Hezbollah rocket hit residential building in Nahariya at ~6 PM. Fire between two homes. 6 wounded (2 adults, 4 children — smoke inhalation). Separate impact near Kibbutz Kabri wounded 1 man.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed rocket from Lebanon, not failed interceptor

title: Fujairah Oil Terminal Hit Again — ADNOC Suspends Loading Operations
description: Iranian drone struck Fujairah Oil Industry Zone for second time in two days. Three separate fires reported. ADNOC suspended operations at UAE's only oil export route outside Hormuz (handles 1.8M bbl/day).
factCheckNote: Confirmed by CNBC, Khaleej Times, Gulf News

title: Trump Delays Xi Summit, Says Israel Won't Use Nukes, Kharg 'Destroyed'
description: Trump delays Beijing summit 'a month or so' due to war. Says Israel 'would never' use nuclear weapons (responding to Sacks). Claims Kharg Island 'literally destroyed' except oil facilities. Says war will be 'wrapped up soon.'
factCheckNote: Kharg Island destruction claim unverified. 'Wrapped up soon' contradicts IDF statement of 3+ more weeks.

title: G5 Nations Warn Israel Against Major Lebanon Ground Offensive
description: Canada, France, Germany, Italy, and UK issued joint statement expressing 'grave concern' about Lebanon violence. Warned ground offensive 'would have devastating humanitarian consequences.' 1M+ displaced. Called for UNSC 1701 implementation.
factCheckNote: Official UK government release confirmed

title: EU Rejects Hormuz Military Role — Kallas Proposes 'Black Sea Model' Oil Corridor
description: EU foreign ministers in Brussels reject extending Aspides mission to Hormuz. Kallas: 'Nobody wants to go actively in this war.' Proposed replicating Black Sea grain corridor for oil transit through Hormuz. Germany's Pistorius: 'This is not our war.'
factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirmed EU ministers' positions

title: Drone Strikes Al-Rasheed Hotel in Baghdad Green Zone
description: Drone struck Al-Rasheed Hotel roof in Baghdad's Green Zone. Hotel houses EU missions and Saudi Embassy. No casualties. Katyusha rockets also intercepted near US Embassy. Kataeb Hezbollah retaliating for al-Askari killing.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, US News, Arab News

title: Kataeb Hezbollah Confirms Death of Security Chief Abu Ali al-Askari
description: Kataeb Hezbollah confirmed al-Askari's death in Baghdad airstrike. Security chief and spokesperson for over a decade. Personally involved in Israeli hostage Elizabeth Tsurkov's captivity. Replaced by Abou Moujahed al-Assaf.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Shafaq News, Jerusalem Post, Times of Israel

title: Iranian Drone Strikes Shah Gas Field — ADNOC Suspends Operations
description: Drone struck Shah natural gas field in Abu Dhabi (ADNOC/Occidental). Fire erupted, operations suspended. Produces ~70,000 bbl/day. Third major UAE energy facility hit on Day 17 (after Dubai airport fuel tank and Fujairah oil zone).
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg, Gulf News

title: UAE Closes Entire Airspace — Exceptional Precautionary Measure
description: UAE GCAA announced temporary full closure of national airspace after series of Iranian attacks on energy infrastructure and airports. Foreign carriers banned. Reopened early Tuesday.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by US News, Bloomberg, WAM

title: Oil Drops as Bessent Says US Allowing Iranian Tankers Through Hormuz
description: Brent fell from $106 to $100.21 after Treasury Secretary Bessent told CNBC the US is allowing Iranian tankers through Hormuz. WSJ reported US will announce Hormuz escort coalition. Bessent said oil should fall 'much lower' than $80 after war ends.
factCheckNote: Bessent interview aired on CNBC

title: NBC: Pentagon Presents Daily Exit Options — Trump Takes None
description: NBC News reported (6 sources) Pentagon includes exit strategy options in daily war planning briefings for Trump, but he has not chosen any. White House initial assessment: 4-6 weeks to achieve military objectives. US intelligence sees no signs of imminent collapse of Iran's establishment.
factCheckNote: Sourced from 6 officials familiar with planning

title: Turkey Condemns Israel's Lebanon Ground Offensive
description: Turkey 'condemns in the strongest terms' Israel's ground offensive in Lebanon, warns it 'further deepens instability.' Accused Netanyahu government of 'genocide and collective punishment.' Official MFA Statement No. 49.
factCheckNote: Official Turkish MFA statement confirmed

title: Massive Overnight Bombardment of Tehran — Saadabad Palace Area
description: Sustained Israeli/US strikes hit northern Tehran near Saadabad Palace from 01:29 to 05:34 local time. 15+ districts reported explosions. Also struck Karaj, Shiraz, Abadan, Khorramshahr, Chabahar.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple independent media and resident accounts

title: Basij Commander Gholamreza Soleimani and Deputy Killed
description: IDF announced killing of Basij paramilitary commander Gen. Gholamreza Soleimani, deputy Seyyed Karishi, and IRGC Aerospace Force chief in targeted strikes on Tehran. Israel says majority of Basij leadership eliminated.
factCheckNote: Israel claims; Iran has not confirmed or denied

title: Israel Claims Killing of Ali Larijani — Iran's De Facto Leader
description: Defense Minister Katz announced Ali Larijani, secretary of Supreme National Security Council and de facto leader since Khamenei's death, killed in overnight IAF strikes on Tehran. Biggest targeted killing since Khamenei. Iran has not confirmed.
factCheckNote: Israel announced; Tasnim posted purported handwritten note from Larijani but dated for earlier funeral

title: Israel Launches Ground Invasion of Southern Lebanon
description: 91st Division enters southern Lebanon creating expanded buffer zone south of Litani River. 6 divisions (~100,000 soldiers) amassed. Katz compared to Gaza war, suggested indefinite occupation. 886 killed in Lebanon, 1M displaced.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by IDF, Lebanese government, and multiple news agencies

title: Iran Fires 4th Missile Salvo With Cluster Munitions at Central Israel
description: Fourth ballistic missile attack since midnight. Cluster munitions damaged train station in central Israel. Fragments near Knesset and Church of the Holy Sepulchre. No injuries reported.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Israeli military and media

title: Most Intense Attack on US Embassy Baghdad
description: US Embassy in Green Zone targeted by 3 drones + 4 rockets — most intense since war began. C-RAM shot down 2, 1 drone struck inside. Drone also hit Al-Rasheed Hotel. 4 killed in retaliatory US strike on PMF building in Jadriya.
factCheckNote: US Embassy issued security alert; confirmed by multiple outlets

title: Pakistani Killed by Missile Debris in Abu Dhabi — UAE Airspace Briefly Closed
description: Falling debris from intercepted Iranian missile killed Pakistani national in Bani Yas, Abu Dhabi. UAE temporarily closed entire airspace (3:14-5:05 AM) as precautionary measure. UAE says 304 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,627 UAVs intercepted since war began.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by UAE government and multiple Gulf outlets

title: Tanker Struck Near Fujairah — Second Oil Zone Drone Strike
description: UKMTO reported tanker hit by unknown projectile 23 NM east of Fujairah. Separately, second drone strike in 3 days hit Fujairah Oil Industry Zone, threatening UAE's last Hormuz-bypass export route.
factCheckNote: UKMTO advisory confirmed; multiple maritime security outlets

title: Trump Delays China Trip — Allies Rebuff Hormuz Coalition
description: Trump delays planned China trip. Called on allies to send warships to Hormuz — Germany, UK, France, Italy all decline. Kallas floats Black Sea grain deal model for Hormuz shipping corridor.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by PBS, NPR, multiple outlets

title: Pakistani national killed by missile debris in Abu Dhabi
description: Falling debris from intercepted Iranian ballistic missile killed a Pakistani national in Abu Dhabi's Bani Yas area. UAE total interceptions since war began: 304 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,627 drones.
factCheckNote: UAE defense ministry confirmed

title: Iran deploys Haj Qasem ballistic missile for first time
description: IRGC used the Haj Qasem missile (named after Qasem Soleimani, 1,400km range, MaRV-equipped to defeat THAAD/Patriot) for the first time in combat. Targeted Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, Beit Shemesh, and US bases across the Gulf in 59th missile attack wave.
factCheckNote: IRGC officially announced deployment

title: Missile fragments hit Jerusalem holy sites — Temple Mount, Holy Sepulchre
description: Israeli police found missile and interceptor fragments at the Temple Mount/Al-Aqsa Mosque, Church of the Holy Sepulchre, Armenian Patriarchate, and Jewish Quarter. A warhead landed less than 1km from the Temple Mount. 1 minor injury.
factCheckNote: Israeli police confirmed debris locations

title: Iran strikes UAE Shah gas field — 20% of UAE gas supply ablaze
description: Iran attacked UAE's Shah gas field, setting it ablaze. The field supplies 20% of UAE natural gas. Fujairah oil terminal hit for the 3rd time in 4 days. Kuwait-flagged tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah also struck near Fujairah.
factCheckNote: CNBC and Bloomberg confirmed attack

title: Joe Kent resigns as NCTC Director — highest-profile war resignation
description: National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent resigned, becoming the highest-profile Trump official to quit over the war. Said Iran 'posed no imminent threat' and blamed 'pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.' Trump called him 'very weak on security.'
factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirmed resignation

title: WSJ: Russia sharing satellite imagery and drone tech with Iran
description: Wall Street Journal reports Russia expanding intelligence sharing with Iran — providing satellite imagery of US force locations and improved Shahed drone components. Russia drawing on Ukraine drone warfare experience.
factCheckNote: Based on WSJ sources including European intelligence officials

title: USS Gerald R. Ford heading to Crete for repairs after onboard fire
description: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) heading to Souda Bay, Crete for 1+ week of repairs after a 30-hour fire. ~200 sailors treated for smoke inhalation, 600 crew lost berthing. Carrier temporarily out of theater.
factCheckNote: USNI News confirmed via Navy sources

title: Kuwait arrests 16-member Hezbollah cell planning sabotage
description: Kuwait arrested 16 Hezbollah-linked cell members (14 Kuwaiti, 2 Lebanese) planning sabotage. Seized weapons, assassination tools, drones, encrypted communications, and ammunition. Hezbollah denied having members in Kuwait.
factCheckNote: Kuwait Interior Ministry official announcement

title: IDF 36th Division pushes deeper into Lebanon; Tyre evacuation warnings
description: IDF's 36th Division began new operations deeper into southern Lebanon, joining the 91st Division's ongoing push. Fresh evacuation warnings issued for Tyre — all civilians told to move north of the Zahrani River.
factCheckNote: IDF spokesperson confirmed operations

title: Trump threatens to leave NATO after allies refuse Hormuz mission
description: Trump said leaving NATO is 'something to think about' after Germany, Japan, Italy, Australia, UK, France, and South Korea refused to join a Hormuz escort coalition. Trump delayed China trip by a month, saying 'We've got a war going on.'
factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirmed Oval Office remarks

title: Witkoff-Araghchi back-channel communication reactivated
description: Direct communication between US envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian FM Araghchi resumed — first direct contact since war began. Contradictory accounts on who initiated. Araghchi publicly denied contact.
factCheckNote: Axios and Drop Site News sources; Araghchi denies

title: Hezbollah rockets hit Nahariya and Karmiel — 6 wounded
description: Hezbollah launched dozens of rockets and 5 drones at northern Israel. Rocket hit residential home in Karmiel. Fire in Nahariya from rocket impact, 6 wounded. 37 Hezbollah attack waves identified on March 16 alone.
factCheckNote: IDF and Israeli emergency services confirmed

title: Projectile strikes near Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
description: A projectile hit premises of Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Plant near the operating unit. IAEA confirmed no damage or injuries. Rosatom condemned strike, Russia evacuating personnel.
factCheckNote: IAEA confirmed no reactor damage

title: Chaharshanbe Suri fire festival — clashes reported across Iran
description: Persian fire festival celebrated under wartime conditions. Opposition called for mass participation. Regime imposed near-total internet blackout. Clashes in multiple areas. Netanyahu urged Iranians to celebrate.
factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirmed celebrations and clashes

title: 3-day-old infant and family killed in Arak airstrike
description: A 3-day-old infant, 2-year-old sister, mother, and grandmother killed in US-Israeli strike on Arak. IRGC said deaths made them 'more determined.'
factCheckNote: Democracy Now confirmed via IRGC statement

title: Kata'ib Hezbollah confirms security chief Abu Ali al-Askari killed
description: Iraq's Kata'ib Hezbollah confirmed senior security commander Abu Ali al-Askari killed in US airstrike. He was intelligence chief and involved in captivity of Elizabeth Tsurkov.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Kata'ib Hezbollah itself

title: US Embassy Baghdad hit by coordinated drone and rocket attack
description: 3 explosive drones launched at US Embassy — 2 intercepted, 1 struck outer wall. Rockets also targeted embassy. C-RAM defenses activated near Baghdad airport. No US casualties.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and Al Arabiya confirmed

title: Israeli strikes kill 17+ across Lebanon — Beirut, Nabatieh, Baalbek
description: 17+ killed: Beirut 6 killed/24 injured, Nabatieh 3 killed/8 missing, Bint Jbeil 3, Baalbek 4, Jebchit 4 Syrians. Lebanese soldier killed. Road near Beirut airport struck.
factCheckNote: Lebanese Health Ministry figures

title: IRGC 'rapid strike' retaliation — 2 killed in Ramat Gan
description: IRGC launched Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr missiles at Israel in retaliation for Larijani killing. Elderly couple killed in Ramat Gan. Shrapnel hit Tel Aviv Savidor Center train station. 5 wounded.
factCheckNote: Israeli emergency services and IRGC both confirmed

title: IDF evacuates Tyre, strikes follow — 11,000 displaced
description: IDF issued mass evacuation order for Tyre and surrounding refugee camps. Strikes hit Tyre and Burj Shemali predawn. Amnesty condemned orders as overly broad.
factCheckNote: Amnesty International confirmed

title: Israeli strikes on Beirut kill 20+ — Bachoura, Basta, Zuqaq al-Blat
description: Multiple strikes across central Beirut. Bachoura building destroyed killing 6. Zuqaq al-Blat and Basta hit without warning, 6 more killed, dozens wounded.
factCheckNote: Lebanese Health Ministry

title: Israel kills Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — third assassination in 48h
description: IDF overnight airstrike killed Iran's Intelligence Minister. Third senior official eliminated after Larijani and Basij chief Soleimani. Katz: 'No one in Iran has immunity — everyone is a target.'
factCheckNote: Confirmed by Israel, Iran says nothing announced

title: Dorud city strike kills 7, injures 56 — Chegeni kills 4 more
description: Strike on densely populated area of Dorud killed 7 and injured 56. Separate strike in Chegeni region killed 4.
factCheckNote: Tasnim confirmed

title: US-Israel strike South Pars gas field — world's largest
description: Israeli jets struck gas storage tanks at South Pars/Assaluyeh. Two refineries halted (~100M cubic meters/day). First upstream energy strike of war. Brent surged 5%.
factCheckNote: Iranian state media and IDF confirmed

title: IMO emergency session on Hormuz safe corridor
description: International Maritime Organisation opened emergency session. 40-member council to vote on safe maritime corridor for evacuating stranded seafarers from Persian Gulf.
factCheckNote: Euronews confirmed

title: US drops 5,000-lb bunker busters on Hormuz missile sites
description: CENTCOM used deep penetrator munitions against hardened Iranian antiship missile sites along Hormuz coastline. Analysts call it opening of multi-week campaign to reopen waterway.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM statement confirmed

title: Iran threatens Saudi, UAE, Qatar energy infrastructure
description: After South Pars strike, Iran named specific Gulf energy targets as legitimate targets: Saudi Samref, Jubail, UAE Al Hasan, Qatar facilities. Gulf states intercepted new missiles/drones.
factCheckNote: Tasnim agency, confirmed by multiple outlets

title: Mojtaba Khamenei rejects ceasefire — 'not the right time for peace'
description: Iran's new supreme leader rejected de-escalation proposals from two intermediary countries. Demanded US and Israel be 'brought to their knees' and pay compensation before negotiations.
factCheckNote: India TV News, Free Malaysia Today confirmed

title: Iran executes Swedish citizen accused of spying for Israel
description: Iran executed Swedish national accused of being Israeli spy. Sweden's FM condemned execution.
factCheckNote: Swedish FM confirmed

title: Iranian missile kills 3 Palestinians near Hebron
description: Iranian missile hit beauty salon near Hebron. 3 killed including 2 women. Notable collateral damage on Palestinian territory.
factCheckNote: Haaretz confirmed

title: UAE intercepts 13 ballistic missiles and 27 drones from Iran
description: UAE air defenses intercepted massive Iranian salvo. Projectile hit near Al Minhad Air Base. Since war: 327 ballistic + 1,699 drones intercepted.
factCheckNote: Gulf News confirmed

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts 4 ballistic missiles over Riyadh
description: 4 ballistic missiles intercepted over Riyadh. 21 drones intercepted over Eastern Province. 4 foreign workers injured by debris.
factCheckNote: Saudi Gazette confirmed

title: Iran strikes Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility — world's largest
description: Iranian missiles struck Ras Laffan (20% of global LNG). QatarEnergy: 'extensive damage.' 1 missile hit after 4 intercepted. Qatar expelled Iran's military attaches within 24 hours.
factCheckNote: QatarEnergy + Al Jazeera confirmed

title: Gabbard testifies Iran NOT rebuilding enrichment before war
description: DNI Gabbard omitted prepared statement that Iran had NOT restarted enrichment — undercutting war justification. Called regime 'intact but largely degraded.' IAEA reveals new underground site at Isfahan.
factCheckNote: Bloomberg found discrepancy between written/spoken testimony

title: Israeli strikes kill 35+ across Lebanon — Al Manar TV director among dead
description: 35+ killed, 61+ wounded in strikes across Baalbek, Sahmar, Jibchit, and other areas. Hezbollah's Al Manar TV director killed in Beirut. Total Lebanon toll now ~968.
factCheckNote: Anadolu Agency confirmed

title: Trump attends dignified transfer at Dover AFB for 6 killed in KC-135 crash
description: Trump flew to Dover for dignified transfer of 6 service members killed in KC-135 refueling aircraft crash over Iraq. Accompanied by Hegseth, Speaker Johnson.
factCheckNote: AP confirmed

title: Iranian cluster missile kills elderly couple in Ramat Gan; foreign worker killed at Moshav Adanim
description: Iranian Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr cluster missiles strike central Israel. Elderly couple Yaron and Ilana Moshe killed in Ramat Gan apartment. 30-year-old foreign worker killed at Moshav Adanim. Shrapnel damages Tel Aviv train station. Israel death toll rises to 17.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel, Haaretz confirmed

title: IRGC names 5 Gulf energy facilities as imminent targets after South Pars strike
description: IRGC declares Saudi SAMREF refinery, Jubail petrochemical, UAE Al Hosn gasfield, Qatar Ras Laffan and Mesaieed 'direct and legitimate targets' to be struck 'in coming hours.' Warns all workers to evacuate. Saudi Aramco evacuates SAMREF. Major energy firms begin non-essential staff evacuation.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Middle East Eye, Anadolu confirmed

title: Abu Dhabi suspends Habshan gas operations after missile interception debris
description: Gas operations at Abu Dhabi's Habshan gas complex and Bab oil field temporarily suspended after debris from intercepted Iranian missiles fell on facilities. UAE air defenses intercepted 13 ballistic missiles and 27 drones. No injuries reported.
factCheckNote: Abu Dhabi Media Office confirmed

title: IDF destroys two Litani River bridges, isolating south Lebanon
description: Israeli military destroys two bridges over Litani River, cutting off southern Lebanon from the rest of the country. ~150,000 civilians trapped south of river. IDF orders evacuation south of Zahrani River. Part of campaign to disrupt Hezbollah weapons smuggling routes.
factCheckNote: Asharq Al-Awsat, Haaretz confirmed

title: IMO opens emergency session on Hormuz shipping crisis
description: UN maritime body opens extraordinary council session in London. Secretary-General Dominguez calls situation 'unacceptable.' 3,200 ships trapped, 20,000 seafarers stranded, 7 merchant sailors killed. Safe maritime corridor proposal backed by Bahrain, Japan, Panama, Singapore, UAE and US. Vote expected Thursday.
factCheckNote: Euronews, CNBC confirmed

title: Federal Reserve holds rates at 3.5-3.75%; warns inflation not falling as hoped
description: Fed keeps rates steady for second consecutive meeting. Powell warns inflation 'not coming down as hoped.' Projects only one rate cut in 2026, raises inflation outlook to 2.7%. Dow drops 768 points (-1.63%), S&P 500 -1.36%, Nasdaq -1.46%. Gas prices at $3.84/gallon.
factCheckNote: CNBC, CNN confirmed

title: Pentagon requests $200B+ war supplemental from Congress
description: Pentagon asks White House to approve over $200 billion supplemental budget request to fund Iran war. Would urgently increase production of critical weaponry expended over 3-week campaign. First 6 days cost ~$11.3B. Expected to face resistance from lawmakers opposed to conflict.
factCheckNote: Washington Post confirmed via senior administration official

title: Riyadh emergency FM meeting — missile alert during session
description: 10+ Arab/Islamic foreign ministers met without US. Missile alerts sounded in Riyadh during meeting.
factCheckNote: US News confirmed

title: Germany's Merz: bombing Iran 'futile' for regime change
description: Chancellor Merz shifts to open criticism. Warns of perpetual war, refuses Hormuz ops, says NATO is defensive not offensive.
factCheckNote: The Hill, DW confirmed

title: Trump issues 60-day Jones Act waiver for energy shipping
description: Foreign-flagged vessels now allowed to transport oil/gas between US ports. Treasury also eases Venezuela PDVSA sanctions.
factCheckNote: CNBC confirmed

title: Senate defeats third war powers resolution 53-47
description: Booker resolution to halt Iran war defeated. Fetterman voted with GOP, Rand Paul with Dems. Democrats pledge weekly votes.
factCheckNote: CBS News confirmed

title: Fed holds rates — Dow drops 768, PPI jumps 0.7%
description: Fed holds at 3.5-3.75%. Powell: oil disruption 'likely short-lived.' Only 1 cut projected for 2026. Dow -768 (-1.63%), hot PPI at +0.7% vs +0.3% expected.
factCheckNote: CNBC confirmed

title: Israeli drones kill 3 Lebanese Army soldiers
description: Drone strikes killed 3 LAF soldiers in Nabatieh/Zibdin/Doueir. President Aoun condemned. Israel says 'under review.'
factCheckNote: The National confirmed

title: Foreign worker killed at Moshav Adanim by cluster missile
description: 30-year-old killed by Iranian cluster missile shrapnel. 4+ injured in Bnei Brak, Kafr Qassem, Jaljulia. Israel death toll now 20.
factCheckNote: MDA confirmed

title: Iran fires 6+ missile salvos at Israel overnight — cluster bombs
description: IRGC fired at least 6 separate ballistic missile salvos with cluster warheads at central/northern Israel. Most hit open areas.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed salvos

title: Trump: 'NO MORE' South Pars strikes, but threatens massive US response if Qatar hit again
description: Trump posted on Truth Social. Axios: Trump DID coordinate original strike with Netanyahu despite denying it.
factCheckNote: Axios contradicts Trump's claim US 'knew nothing'

title: Ras Laffan struck second time — fires and extensive damage
description: Iran hit Qatar's Ras Laffan a second time in 12 hours. QatarEnergy: 'sizeable fires and extensive further damage.'
factCheckNote: QatarEnergy confirmed

title: Saudi FM: 'Trust in Iran completely shattered'
description: Prince Faisal declared trust in Iran 'completely shattered.' Riyadh 'reserves right to take military action.'
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera confirmed

title: Asian markets crash — Nikkei -2.7%, Brent breaks $110
description: Nikkei -2.7%, KOSPI -2.6%, Hang Seng -1.4%. Brent crude breaches $110. Dubai crude hits all-time high $150+ for Asian buyers.
factCheckNote: Fortune confirmed

title: Israel strikes Iran's South Pars gas field — 4 treatment plants offline
description: IAF strikes four gas treatment facilities at South Pars/Asaluyeh in Bushehr Province, coordinated with US. Plants taken offline to contain fires. South Pars holds 36% of Iran's gas reserves.
factCheckNote: Bloomberg, NPR confirmed

title: IDF first-ever Caspian Sea strike — Bandar Anzali naval base destroyed
description: IDF Air Force strikes Iranian Navy vessels at Bandar Anzali on the Caspian Sea. 4 missile boats, a corvette, and naval infrastructure destroyed. First IDF strikes in northern Iran, targeting Iran-Russia supply lifeline.
factCheckNote: Israel Hayom, Ynet confirmed

title: Pentagon submits $200B war supplemental request to Congress
description: Pentagon asks White House to approve $200+ billion supplemental budget for Iran war — largest military supplemental since Iraq/Afghanistan. Led by Deputy SecDef Feinberg to address munitions shortage.
factCheckNote: Washington Post, AP confirmed

title: DNI Gabbard: Iran's nuclear enrichment 'obliterated' — was not rebuilding before war
description: DNI Tulsi Gabbard testifies to Senate Intelligence Committee that Iran's nuclear enrichment was obliterated by June 2025 strikes with no efforts to rebuild, contradicting Trump's stated war rationale. Confirms Mojtaba Khamenei 'severely injured.'
factCheckNote: WaPo, Al Jazeera confirmed

title: Israel kills Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — 3rd assassination in 48 hours
description: Israeli overnight airstrike kills Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, the third senior Iranian official assassinated in 48 hours. President Pezeshkian confirms the death.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, WaPo confirmed

title: Iran revenge missile strike kills couple in Ramat Gan apartment
description: Iran launches revenge missile strikes on Israel. Couple in their 70s killed in Ramat Gan apartment when they couldn't reach safe room. Several others injured across central Israel.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Haaretz confirmed

title: Israeli strikes kill 11 civilians in western Iran — Dorud and Chegeni
description: Israeli strikes in Lorestan Province kill 7 in Dorud and 4 in Chegeni, injuring 56. Multiple residential areas impacted.
factCheckNote: CNN confirmed

title: Iran retaliates on ALL Gulf energy — Ras Laffan, SAMREF, Habshan, Kuwait refineries hit
description: Iran attacks energy facilities across all 6 GCC countries for first time. Qatar Ras Laffan extensive damage, Shell Pearl GTL hit, Saudi SAMREF refinery in Yanbu struck, 2 Kuwaiti refineries ablaze, UAE Habshan shut down. 2 killed in Saudi Al-Kharj.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera, Military.com, CNBC confirmed

title: Qatar expels Iranian military attaches after Ras Laffan attack
description: Qatar declares Iran's military and security attaches persona non grata, ordering them out within 24 hours. Gulf diplomatic isolation of Iran deepens.
factCheckNote: Euronews, The National confirmed

title: Senate blocks war powers resolution 53-47 — third failed vote
description: Senate Republicans block Sen. Booker's war powers resolution. Rand Paul only GOP vote for; Fetterman only Dem vote against. Third failed war powers vote since Feb 28.
factCheckNote: CBS News, The Hill confirmed

title: EU summit refuses to join US military campaign or secure Hormuz
description: EU leaders in Brussels refuse to join US/Israeli military campaign or help secure Strait of Hormuz. Spanish PM: 'We are against this war because it is illegal.' EU foreign policy chief Kallas: 'no appetite' to expand naval presence.
factCheckNote: WaPo, Euronews confirmed

title: Hegseth: US launching 'largest strike package yet' — 7,000+ targets hit
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth announces largest strike package yet. 64 Apache helicopters deployed vs Iranian drones. 5,000-lb bunker busters used on hardened Hormuz missile sites. Over 7,000 targets struck total.
factCheckNote: CBS News confirmed

title: US/Israeli strikes hit Iraq PMF in Salahuddin and Nineveh — 2 killed
description: Dawn airstrikes hit PMF 6th and 31st Brigades in Beiji and PMF 4th Regiment in Nineveh Plains. Islamic Resistance in Iraq claims 290+ attacks on US forces since Feb 28.
factCheckNote: The National confirmed

title: Israeli bombardment kills 33 across Lebanon — Litani bridges destroyed
description: Strikes across Lebanon kill 33, wound 152. At least 2 Litani River bridges destroyed. Bashoura, central Beirut struck. Total Lebanon toll surpasses 1,000.
factCheckNote: Haaretz, Al Jazeera confirmed

title: HIMARS fires from Bahrain at Iran — first US missiles from Gulf state
description: NBC verifies video of US HIMARS launchers firing from Bahrain at Iranian targets. First documented US ballistic missiles launched from a Gulf state.
factCheckNote: NBC News verified video

title: US F-35 hit by Iranian fire — first hostile damage to F-35 in history
description: USAF F-35 struck by suspected Iranian fire during combat mission, forced emergency landing. Pilot stable. CENTCOM investigating. First confirmed hostile hit on F-35.
factCheckNote: CENTCOM confirmed emergency landing

title: Haifa Bazan oil refinery hit by intercepted missile shrapnel
description: Shrapnel from intercepted Iranian missile struck Bazan Group oil refinery in Haifa, sparking fires extinguished quickly. 45-minute power outage in Haifa area. No injuries.
factCheckNote: IDF confirmed intercepted missile fragments, not a direct hit

title: Iranian cluster munitions kill 3 Palestinian women in West Bank, foreign worker in central Israel
description: 3 Palestinian women killed in southern West Bank when Iranian missile fragments struck a hair salon. A 30-year-old foreign worker killed at Moshav Adanim by cluster bomb shrapnel. Rising civilian toll from Iranian cluster munitions.
factCheckNote: Times of Israel confirmed with MDA and IDF

title: Rockets strike Kiryat Shmona — 4 wounded including 1 serious
description: Hezbollah rockets struck Kiryat Shmona, with one hitting a building. MDA treated 4 wounded: 60-year-old man (serious), 68-year-old woman (moderate), two young men (light).
factCheckNote: IDF and MDA confirmed casualties

title: Macron calls Trump and Qatar Emir — urges moratorium on civilian infrastructure strikes
description: French President Macron spoke with Trump and the Emir of Qatar, urging a moratorium on strikes targeting civilian infrastructure, especially water and energy facilities.
factCheckNote: Reported by Haaretz from Elysee statement

title: Gabbard faces House Intelligence Committee — dodges nuclear threat question again
description: DNI Gabbard testified before House Intelligence Committee alongside CIA Director Ratcliffe and FBI Director Patel. Declined to directly answer whether she assessed there was no imminent nuclear threat from Iran before the war. Separate from March 18 Senate hearing.
factCheckNote: NBC, Democracy Now confirmed testimony details

title: Six Western allies issue joint Hormuz safe passage statement
description: UK, France, Germany, Japan, and two additional allies declared readiness to 'contribute to appropriate efforts to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.' UK sent military planners to CENTCOM. ~20,000 seafarers on ~3,200 vessels remain stranded.
factCheckNote: Multiple governments confirmed; reported by CNN, Bloomberg

title: Netanyahu evening press conference — claims Iran can no longer enrich uranium
description: Netanyahu claimed Iran 'can no longer enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles.' Said Israel needs 'ground component' for regime change. Confirmed Israel acted alone on South Pars. Said war ending 'a lot faster than people think.' Addressed Iranian people: 'We are standing by your side.'
factCheckNote: No independent evidence provided for uranium enrichment claim; IAEA chief Grossi says 'material will still be there'

title: UNSC adopts Resolution 2817 condemning Iran's Gulf attacks (13-0-2)
description: UN Security Council adopted Resolution 2817 condemning Iran's attacks against Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Jordan. 13 in favor, 0 against, 2 abstentions (China, Russia). First formal UN condemnation of Iran's attacks on Gulf neighbors.
factCheckNote: UN official press release SC16315

title: Iran executes 3 men linked to January protests including 19-year-old wrestler
description: Iran carried out public hangings in Qom of three men connected to January 2026 anti-government protests. Among them was a 19-year-old wrestler whose conviction drew US criticism. Human rights groups described trials as shams relying on forced confessions and torture.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple rights organizations

title: IAF conserving interceptors — choosing not to shoot down some Iranian cluster bomblets
description: Israeli Air Force making tactical decisions not to intercept some Iranian cluster submunitions to conserve interceptor missiles. About half of Iran's missiles carry cluster warheads with ~24 submunitions each. Overall interception rate for missiles targeting populated areas: 90%.
factCheckNote: IDF official confirmed to Times of Israel

title: Qatar expels Iranian military/security personnel — 24 hours to leave
description: Qatar declared Iranian military and security forces persona non grata, giving them 24 hours to leave the country. Called the Ras Laffan LNG strike 'a dangerous escalation and flagrant violation of sovereignty.' Qatar had maintained neutrality since Feb 28 but said Iran 'continuously targeted' it.
factCheckNote: Qatar MFA official statement

title: IMO begins negotiations for humanitarian corridor for 20,000 stranded seafarers
description: UN International Maritime Organization announced it will negotiate with countries to establish a humanitarian corridor to free ~20,000 seafarers stranded in the Persian Gulf by the war and Hormuz blockade.
factCheckNote: IMO official announcement

title: Canada joins 7-nation Hormuz coalition — UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan sign joint statement
description: Seven nations issued joint statement expressing readiness to contribute to efforts ensuring safe passage through Strait of Hormuz. Called for immediate moratorium on attacks on civilian infrastructure. Marked significant shift from earlier European reluctance to join naval operations.
factCheckNote: Published on GOV.UK official site

title: IDF strikes 200+ targets — first attack on Bandar Anzali, Caspian Sea coast
description: Israeli Air Force struck over 200 targets in western and central Iran. First-ever strike on Bandar Anzali on the Caspian Sea coast destroyed 5 Iranian Navy missile boats, a headquarters, and a shipyard. Joint Chiefs Chairman Caine said forces striking 'deeper' using 5,000-lb bunker busters.
factCheckNote: Alma Research Center daily report confirmed

title: Hegseth announces 'largest strike package yet' — Pentagon requests $200B war supplemental
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth said US will launch 'largest strike package yet.' US has struck 7,800+ targets, destroyed all 11 Iranian subs, sunk 120+ warships. Pentagon requesting $200B supplemental from Congress. Hegseth: 'It takes money to kill bad guys.' No timeframe set for war's end.
factCheckNote: Pentagon briefing aired live on multiple networks

title: State Department approves arms sales to Gulf countries amid Iranian attacks
description: US State Department approved new arms sales to Gulf nations facing continued Iranian missile and drone attacks on energy infrastructure. Comes as UAE, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Qatar all report intercepting or sustaining Iranian strikes.
factCheckNote: State Dept notification confirmed

title: Joe Kent under FBI probe for classified Iran war leaks — resigned from NCTC
description: FBI investigating former National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent for alleged leaks of classified information about the Iran war. Kent resigned earlier this week over the Trump administration's handling of the conflict.
factCheckNote: Under investigation; outcome pending

title: Bessent: US may unsanction 140M barrels of Iranian oil to flood market and lower prices
description: Treasury Secretary Bessent said US may remove sanctions on ~140M barrels of Iranian oil stranded on tankers. 'Using the Iranian barrels against the Iranians to keep oil prices lower for 10-14 days.' Also flagged potential additional SPR release. China has stopped exporting jet fuel and refined products.
factCheckNote: Confirmed by US News, Axios, Bloomberg

title: Airstrikes hit Iraq PMF brigades — 3 fighters killed in Salahuddin and Nineveh
description: Strikes hit PMF 6th and 31st Brigades at Beiji HQ and Al Siniya checkpoint (2 killed), and PMF 30th Brigade in Nineveh Plains (1 killed, 2 injured). Continues pattern of targeting Iranian-backed militias in Iraq.
factCheckNote: The National confirmed

title: IDF kills 20+ Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon — Golani Brigade engaged
description: IDF killed 20+ Hezbollah fighters in 24 hours. Golani Brigade engaged anti-tank missile teams (5 killed by troops, 3 by IAF). IDF issued evacuation order south of Zahrani River. Confiscated RPGs, anti-tank rockets.
factCheckNote: IDF spokesperson confirmed

title: IRGC Spokesperson Gen. Ali Mohammad Naeini killed in overnight Israeli strike
description: IDF killed IRGC spokesperson Brig. Gen. Naeini in airstrike on Tehran/Karaj command centers. IRGC confirmed his death. He was the 4th senior Iranian leader killed this week (after Larijani, Soleimani, Khatib). He had hours earlier claimed Iran still producing missiles.
factCheckNote: IRGC confirmed; IDF confirmed strike

title: Israel launches Nowruz wave of strikes on Tehran
description: IDF announced 'wave of strikes targeting infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime' in Tehran on Nowruz (Persian New Year). Multiple explosions reported across the capital as Iranians marked the holiday.
factCheckNote: IDF spokesperson confirmed; CNN, Haaretz verified

title: Iran drones hit Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery for second consecutive day
description: Two waves of Iranian drone strikes hit Kuwait's largest refinery (730K bpd), sparking fires. Several units shut down. Second consecutive day of attacks on this facility.
factCheckNote: Kuwait MOD confirmed; AP, Argus Media reported

title: Iranian drone strikes Saudi SAMREF refinery at Yanbu
description: Drone struck SAMREF refinery (Saudi Aramco/ExxonMobil JV, 400K bpd) in Yanbu. Saudi FM warned restraint 'is not unlimited.' Air defenses intercepted ballistic missile targeting Yanbu Port and additional drones over Riyadh.
factCheckNote: Saudi MOD confirmed; Al Arabiya, Saudi Gazette reported

title: UAE air defenses intercept Iranian fire over Dubai on Eid
description: Heavy explosions shook Dubai as UAE air defenses intercepted incoming Iranian missiles/drones during Eid al-Fitr. Dubai Media Office confirmed successful interceptions.
factCheckNote: Dubai Media Office confirmed

title: Netanyahu agrees to halt energy site strikes after Trump demand
description: Netanyahu said Israel will comply with Trump's demand to stop attacking Iran energy infrastructure. 'Israel acted alone against Asaluyeh. President Trump asked us to hold off.' Also claimed Iran can no longer enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles.
factCheckNote: Press conference broadcast live; multiple outlets confirmed

title: Pentagon requests $200B supplemental war budget from Congress
description: Pentagon sent $200+ billion supplemental request to White House for Iran war funding. Hegseth: 'That number could move. It takes money to kill bad guys.' CSIS estimates $16.5B spent in first 12 days. GOP divisions emerging — Freedom Caucus wants it 'paid for.'
factCheckNote: WaPo, Fortune, NPR independently confirmed

title: Mojtaba Khamenei Nowruz statement — still not seen in public
description: Iran's Supreme Leader issued Nowruz message calling for enemies' 'security to be taken away.' Read by TV anchor with still photo — Mojtaba has still not appeared in person since assuming power. US officials suggest he may be injured/disfigured.
factCheckNote: Statement confirmed; injury claims unverified

title: Araghchi rejects ceasefire — 'we do not want this scenario repeated'
description: Iran FM Araghchi rejected ceasefire on Al Jazeera: 'We are not seeking a cease-fire because we do not want this scenario to be repeated again.' On nuclear material: 'under the rubbles' with 'no plan to recover.' Negotiations 'off the table.'
factCheckNote: Interview broadcast on Al Jazeera; Time magazine reported

title: European Council calls for moratorium on energy/water facility strikes
description: All 27 EU heads of state called for de-escalation, moratorium on strikes against energy and water facilities, reopening of Strait of Hormuz. Kallas: 'no appetite' to join military campaign. ECB warns of inflation impact.
factCheckNote: European Council conclusions published officially

title: DNI Gabbard: Iran was NOT rebuilding enrichment before war
description: Tulsi Gabbard testified Iran regime 'intact but largely degraded.' Intel assessed Iran NOT rebuilding enrichment — undercutting Trump justification. Joe Kent resigned from NCTC, saying Iran 'posed no imminent threat.'
factCheckNote: Senate testimony; Foreign Policy, WaPo confirmed

title: USS Boxer ARG + 11th MEU departs San Diego for Middle East
description: USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group with 11th MEU (~4,000 personnel, F-35s) departed San Diego ahead of schedule for Middle East. Second MEU deployment to theater, joining ~50,000 US troops already in region.
factCheckNote: Pentagon confirmed; JPost, ABC News reported

title: Bahrain warehouse fire from intercepted Iranian shrapnel
description: Fire erupted at Bahrain warehouse from shrapnel of intercepted Iranian projectile. No injuries. Bahrain has intercepted 139 missiles and 238 drones since war began.
factCheckNote: Bahrain Interior Ministry confirmed

title: Saudi intercepts missile over Al Jouf; Brent crude hits $110.71
description: Saudi air defenses intercepted Iranian missile over northern Al Jouf region. Oil markets volatile — Brent at $110.71 after briefly touching $119.11. Triple-witching day with $5.7T in options expiring.
factCheckNote: Al Jazeera confirmed Saudi intercept

title: IRGC spokesman Naini killed in Nowruz strikes
description: Brigadier General Ali Mohammad Naini, IRGC spokesman since 2024, was killed in an Israeli strike 'at dawn' on Nowruz.
